turns.text: 2025 was a transformative year for Alphabet. Full-year Google Cloud revenue was $56.8 billion, up 29% and ahead of our raised guidance of $55 billion. Full-year total revenue crossed $400 billion for the first time. Our AI investments are clearly delivering returns. For 2026, we expect Google Cloud to grow at least 25% and are targeting an operating margin above 38% for the full company. Gemini is now integrated into every major Google product used by more than 100 million people.

Q4 revenues were $111.8 billion, up 14% year-over-year. Full-year operating income was $130.9 billion with a margin of 33%. Google Cloud Q4 revenue was $16.2 billion, up 28%. Capital expenditures for the full year were $55.1 billion, in line with guidance. We repurchased $57.5 billion of shares in 2025 and are authorizing an additional $70 billion buyback program.
Our next question comes from Eric Sheridan at Goldman Sachs.
Sundar, with full year revenue crossing $400 billion, how are you thinking about capital allocation priorities for 2026 between AI infrastructure and buybacks?

statements.claim: Full-year Google Cloud revenue was $56.8 billion in 2025, up 29% and ahead of the $55 billion target.
Google Cloud will grow at least 25% in 2026.
Alphabet is targeting an operating margin above 38% for the full company in 2026.
Alphabet is authorizing an additional $70 billion share repurchase program.
Gemini is integrated into every major Google product used by more than 100 million people.
